text: To reset your password: 1) Click your avatar in the top-right corner, 2) Select 'Account Settings', 3) Click 'Security' tab, 4) Click 'Reset Password', 5) Enter your current password, then your new password twice, 6) Click 'Update Password'. You'll receive a confirmation email within 2 minutes. If you've forgotten your current password, use the 'Forgot Password?' link on the login page to receive a reset link via email.

API rate limits depend on your plan: Free plan — 100 requests/minute, 1,000 requests/day. Pro plan — 1,000 requests/minute, 50,000 requests/day. Enterprise plan — 10,000 requests/minute, unlimited daily. When you exceed the limit, you'll receive a 429 Too Many Requests response. The 'X-RateLimit-Remaining' and 'X-RateLimit-Reset' headers show your remaining quota and reset time.

To enable two-factor authentication (2FA): 1) Go to Account Settings → Security, 2) Click 'Enable 2FA', 3) Scan the QR code with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, or Microsoft Authenticator), 4) Enter the 6-digit code to confirm, 5) Save your backup recovery codes in a secure location. If you lose your device, use a recovery code to log in, then reconfigure 2FA.

Billing invoices are generated on the 1st of each month. To view and download invoices: 1) Go to Account Settings → Billing, 2) Click 'Invoice History', 3) Select the month you need, 4) Click 'Download PDF'. Invoices include plan charges, overage fees, and any applied credits. For billing disputes, contact billing@support.com with your invoice number.

If your webhook is failing: 1) Verify the endpoint URL is publicly accessible (not localhost), 2) Ensure your server responds with HTTP 200 within 10 seconds, 3) Check that your SSL certificate is valid (we require HTTPS), 4) Review the delivery logs at Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → Delivery Log. Each failed delivery includes the request body, response, and error message. We retry failed webhooks 3 times with exponential backoff (1 min, 5 min, 30 min).

To configure SSO (Single Sign-On) with your identity provider: 1) Go to Admin Settings → Authentication → SSO, 2) Select your provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, or custom SAML), 3) Enter the Metadata URL or upload the XML file, 4) Map user attributes (email, name, department), 5) Enable 'Enforce SSO' to require all users to log in via SSO. SCIM provisioning is available on Enterprise plans for automatic user sync.

To upgrade or change your plan: 1) Go to Account Settings → Billing → Plans, 2) Compare features across Free, Pro, and Enterprise plans, 3) Click 'Upgrade' on your desired plan, 4) Enter payment details if not already on file, 5) Your new plan takes effect immediately. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing cycle. Prorated credits are applied for mid-cycle upgrades.

To invite team members: 1) Go to Admin Settings → Team, 2) Click 'Invite Member', 3) Enter their email address, 4) Assign a role (Admin, Editor, or Viewer), 5) Click 'Send Invite'. Invitees receive an email with a link to join your workspace. Pending invitations expire after 7 days. Admin role can manage billing and team. Editor can create and edit content. Viewer has read-only access.

To connect your application via API: 1) Generate an API key at Account Settings → API Keys → Create Key, 2) Choose the key scope (read-only, read-write, or admin), 3) Copy the key immediately — it won't be shown again, 4) Add the key to your request headers as 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'. API documentation is available at docs.example.com. All API endpoints use JSON format.

To export your data: 1) Go to Account Settings → Data → Export, 2) Select the data types to export (users, content, analytics, audit logs), 3) Choose the format (CSV, JSON, or Parquet), 4) Click 'Start Export'. Large exports run in the background — you'll receive an email with a download link when ready. Export files are available for 7 days. We support GDPR data portability requests.

If you cannot log in: 1) Verify you're using the correct email address (check for typos), 2) Try resetting your password using the 'Forgot Password' link, 3) If you have 2FA enabled, make sure you're entering the current code (codes refresh every 30 seconds), 4) Check if your account has been locked (5 failed attempts triggers a 15-minute lockout), 5) Clear your browser cache and cookies, 6) Try a private/incognito window. If none of these work, contact support for account recovery.

Our refund policy: We offer a full refund within 14 days of purchase for annual plans, no questions asked. Monthly plans can be cancelled at any time — you retain access until the end of the billing period, but we don't issue partial refunds for monthly plans. To request a refund: 1) Go to Account Settings → Billing → Cancel Plan, 2) Select 'Request Refund', 3) Provide a reason (optional), 4) Refunds are processed within 5-10 business days to your original payment method.

To set up email notifications: 1) Go to Account Settings → Notifications, 2) Toggle on/off the notification types you want (ticket updates, weekly digest, system alerts, billing reminders), 3) Set your preferred frequency (instant, daily digest, weekly digest), 4) Optionally add additional email recipients for billing and security alerts. Push notifications for mobile are available in the iOS and Android apps.

Data security: All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). We host on AWS in US-East-1 and EU-West-1 regions — you can choose your data region during onboarding. We are SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant. Audit logs are retained for 1 year and accessible at Admin Settings → Security → Audit Log. We perform annual third-party penetration testing.

To integrate with Slack: 1) Go to Settings → Integrations → Slack, 2) Click 'Connect to Slack', 3) Authorize the app in your Slack workspace, 4) Select the default channel for notifications, 5) Configure which events trigger Slack messages (new tickets, escalations, resolutions). You can also use '/support' slash command in Slack to create tickets directly.

For custom domain setup: 1) Go to Admin Settings → Custom Domain, 2) Enter your domain (e.g., support.yourcompany.com), 3) Add the provided CNAME record to your DNS provider, 4) Wait for DNS propagation (up to 48 hours), 5) We automatically provision and renew SSL certificates via Let's Encrypt. Custom domains are available on Pro and Enterprise plans.

Troubleshooting slow dashboard: 1) Check your internet connection speed, 2) Clear browser cache (Ctrl+Shift+Delete), 3) Disable browser extensions (ad blockers can interfere), 4) Try a different browser (we recommend Chrome or Firefox), 5) If loading a large dataset, try applying filters to reduce the result set, 6) Check our status page at status.example.com for any ongoing incidents.

To delete your account: 1) Go to Account Settings → Danger Zone → Delete Account, 2) Type your account email to confirm, 3) Click 'Permanently Delete'. This action is irreversible — all data, integrations, and team members will be removed. We retain anonymized analytics data for 30 days per our privacy policy. You'll receive a confirmation email after deletion.

If you are not receiving emails from us (verification, password reset, invoices): 1) Check your spam or junk folder, 2) Add support@sentinel-ai.support to your contacts or allowlist, 3) If using a corporate email, ask your IT team to whitelist our sending domain, 4) Make sure your mailbox is not full, 5) Check Account Settings → Notifications to confirm email is enabled. If the problem persists, contact support with an alternative email address.

Payment declined troubleshooting: 1) Verify your card details (number, expiry, CVV) are correct at Account Settings → Billing → Payment Methods, 2) Check with your bank — some banks block international or recurring online charges, 3) Try a different card or payment method (we accept Visa, Mastercard, Amex, and PayPal), 4) Ensure your billing address matches what your bank has on file, 5) If your account is on a free trial, you may need to add a payment method before the trial ends. Charges appear as 'Sentinel Support' on your statement.

To remove or revoke a team member: 1) Go to Admin Settings → Team, 2) Find the member in the list, 3) Click the '...' menu next to their name, 4) Select 'Remove Member'. Their access is revoked immediately. Any content they created remains in your workspace. If they were the sole billing admin, you must assign a new billing admin before removing them. You can re-invite removed members at any time.

API authentication errors (401 Unauthorized): 1) Check your API key is correct and hasn't been rotated — go to Account Settings → API Keys to verify, 2) Make sure you're passing the key in the Authorization header as 'Bearer YOUR_KEY', not as a query parameter, 3) Check that the key has the correct scope for the endpoint you're calling (read-only keys can't write), 4) API keys do not expire, but they can be revoked — if revoked, generate a new one. Test with: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_KEY' https://api.example.com/v1/ping

API error codes reference: 400 Bad Request — invalid request body or missing required fields, check your JSON payload. 401 Unauthorized — invalid or missing API key. 403 Forbidden — your key doesn't have permission for this action. 404 Not Found — the resource doesn't exist or was deleted. 409 Conflict — duplicate resource, e.g. creating a user that already exists. 422 Unprocessable Entity — validation error, response body contains field-level errors. 429 Too Many Requests — rate limit exceeded, check X-RateLimit-Reset header. 500 Internal Server Error — our fault, check status.example.com and try again in a few minutes.

Webhook signature verification: To verify that webhook events are genuinely from us, we sign every request with an HMAC-SHA256 signature. 1) Find your webhook secret at Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → your endpoint → 'Show Secret', 2) Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using the secret, 3) Compare it to the 'X-Webhook-Signature' header (hex-encoded). If they don't match, reject the request. Never process webhooks without verifying the signature — replay attacks are common.

To connect with Zapier: 1) Go to zapier.com and create a new Zap, 2) Search for 'Sentinel Support' as the trigger or action app, 3) Click 'Connect Account' and enter your API key from Account Settings → API Keys, 4) Choose your trigger event (e.g. New Ticket, Ticket Resolved), 5) Map the fields to your target app (Google Sheets, Slack, HubSpot, etc.), 6) Test and activate the Zap. Over 5,000 apps are available via Zapier. For advanced use cases, use our native webhooks instead.

To connect with HubSpot: 1) Go to Settings → Integrations → HubSpot, 2) Click 'Connect HubSpot', 3) Authorize the connection in your HubSpot account, 4) Map customer email to HubSpot Contact, 5) Choose which events sync to HubSpot (new tickets, escalations, resolutions), 6) Optionally sync HubSpot deal stages to ticket status. Requires HubSpot Professional or higher. Data syncs in real-time both ways.

How to use the API pagination: All list endpoints return paginated results. Pass 'page' (default 1) and 'per_page' (default 20, max 100) as query parameters. The response includes 'meta.total', 'meta.page', 'meta.per_page', and 'meta.total_pages'. To get all results, loop until 'meta.page >= meta.total_pages'. Example: GET /v1/tickets?page=2&per_page=50. For high-volume exports, use the Data Export feature instead of API pagination.

How to use webhooks for real-time events: Instead of polling the API, subscribe to webhook events to receive real-time notifications. Available events: ticket.created, ticket.updated, ticket.resolved, ticket.escalated, user.created, user.deleted. To subscribe: 1) Go to Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → Add Endpoint, 2) Enter your HTTPS URL, 3) Select event types, 4) Save. Each event payload includes an 'event_type', 'timestamp', and the full object. Failed deliveries are retried up to 3 times.

Troubleshooting: page not loading or showing blank screen: 1) Hard refresh: Cmd+Shift+R (Mac) or Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows), 2) Clear browser cache and cookies for this site, 3) Disable browser extensions, especially ad blockers and privacy tools, 4) Try Chrome or Firefox — we don't support Internet Explorer, 5) Check if your browser is up to date, 6) Disable VPN or proxy temporarily — some networks block API calls, 7) Check status.example.com for active incidents. If the issue persists, share your browser console errors (F12 → Console tab) with support.

Troubleshooting: file upload failing: 1) Check the file size — maximum upload size is 25MB. For larger files, split or compress them, 2) Supported formats: PDF, PNG, JPG, GIF, CSV, XLSX, DOCX, ZIP. Files outside these formats are rejected, 3) Check your internet connection — large uploads on slow connections may timeout, 4) Try a different browser, 5) If uploading via API, ensure you're using multipart/form-data and the Content-Type header is set correctly, 6) Check if your storage quota is full at Account Settings → Data → Storage.

Troubleshooting: search not returning results: 1) Search indexes update every 5 minutes — very recent content may not appear yet, 2) Check your search query for typos, 3) Remove special characters from the search query, 4) Search is case-insensitive but requires at least 3 characters, 5) Check if you have active filters (date range, category, status) that may be excluding results, 6) If searching via API, make sure you're URL-encoding the query parameter. If you believe content should appear but doesn't, contact support with the search term and content ID.

How to change your email address: 1) Go to Account Settings → Profile → Email Address, 2) Enter your new email and click 'Update Email', 3) A verification link is sent to the new address — click it within 24 hours to confirm, 4) Until confirmed, your old email stays active. If you no longer have access to the old email, contact support with proof of identity. Note: If SSO is enforced in your workspace, your email is managed by your identity provider and cannot be changed here.

How to manage workspaces: You can be a member of multiple workspaces (e.g., different clients or departments). To switch: click the workspace name in the top-left → select a workspace from the dropdown. To create a new workspace: click '+ New Workspace' in the dropdown. Each workspace has independent billing, members, and data. To leave a workspace: Account Settings → Workspaces → find the workspace → 'Leave'. You cannot leave if you are the sole owner — transfer ownership first.

Onboarding checklist for new accounts: 1) Complete your profile (name, avatar, timezone) at Account Settings → Profile, 2) Invite your team members at Admin Settings → Team, 3) Set up your first integration (Slack, HubSpot, or Zapier) at Settings → Integrations, 4) Configure email notifications at Account Settings → Notifications, 5) Review your plan limits at Account Settings → Billing → Plans, 6) Optionally set up SSO if your company uses Okta or Azure AD, 7) Explore the API docs at docs.example.com to start automating workflows.

How to transfer account ownership: 1) Go to Admin Settings → Team, 2) Find the member you want to promote, 3) Click '...' → 'Transfer Ownership', 4) Confirm with your password. The new owner gets full admin rights including billing. You become an Admin (not owner). There can only be one owner per workspace. If the current owner is no longer accessible, contact support with proof of ownership (original registration email, billing records).

Understanding audit logs: Audit logs record all admin actions in your workspace — who did what and when. To access: Admin Settings → Security → Audit Log. Events logged include: login/logout, member invite/remove, role changes, billing changes, SSO configuration, API key creation/deletion, data exports, and permission changes. Logs are retained for 365 days. You can filter by event type, user, or date range. Export audit logs to CSV for compliance reporting. Available on Pro and Enterprise plans.

Data residency and GDPR compliance: We offer data residency in US (us-east-1) and EU (eu-west-1) — choose during onboarding or contact support to migrate. For GDPR: 1) To submit a data subject access request (DSAR), go to Account Settings → Privacy → Request My Data, 2) For data deletion requests, go to Account Settings → Privacy → Request Data Deletion — this permanently removes all personal data within 30 days, 3) We serve as a Data Processor; you are the Data Controller — a DPA is available in Settings → Privacy → Data Processing Agreement. We are GDPR, CCPA, and SOC 2 Type II compliant.

Mobile app troubleshooting: Our iOS and Android apps require iOS 15+ or Android 10+. Common issues: 1) App crashing on launch — update to the latest version from the App Store or Google Play, 2) Push notifications not arriving — go to phone Settings → Notifications → Sentinel Support and enable notifications, 3) Can't log in on mobile — if your org uses SSO, tap 'Login with SSO' and enter your company email, 4) Data not syncing — pull down to refresh, or log out and back in, 5) Slow performance — clear the app cache in phone Settings → Apps → Sentinel Support → Clear Cache.

How to use the bulk operations API: For bulk creating, updating, or deleting records, use the /v1/bulk endpoints to avoid making hundreds of individual API calls. POST /v1/bulk/tickets accepts an array of up to 500 ticket objects. Each item in the response array has a 'status' (success/error) and 'id' or 'error_message'. Bulk operations are processed synchronously for up to 100 items. Over 100 items, the request is queued — you'll receive a job_id and can poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} for status. Bulk operations count against your rate limit as a single request.

How to use API webhooks vs polling: Polling (repeatedly calling GET endpoints) is inefficient and burns your rate limit. Use webhooks instead for real-time updates. Register a webhook endpoint at Settings → Integrations → Webhooks. For events that happen rarely (e.g. user deletion), webhooks save thousands of API calls per day. If your server is temporarily down, we queue and retry missed events for up to 72 hours. For audit or compliance use cases where you need guaranteed delivery, combine webhooks with a periodic full sync via the API once per hour.

Enterprise plan features: Enterprise plans include: unlimited team members, unlimited API requests, dedicated CSM (Customer Success Manager), SLA guarantees (99.99% uptime), custom contract and invoicing, SAML SSO + SCIM provisioning, IP allowlist for API access, private cloud/on-premise deployment options, custom data retention policies, and priority 24/7 phone support. To get an Enterprise quote, contact sales@example.com or fill out the form at example.com/enterprise. Typical setup takes 2-3 business days.
